overall_assessment: The corrected-baseline rerun packet shows strong regression coverage, realistic real-backend evidence, and materially improved assertion depth across resolver, reset-lifecycle, policy, and risk-matrix behavior. Test quality is acceptable for this rerun lane, with one remaining material concern: the authority packet still lacks preserved fail-first provenance for this behavior-defining security hardening slice.
recommended_path: Accept the corrected-baseline rerun as substantively sufficient on test effectiveness and evidence realism, keep the missing fail-first provenance labeled as explicit accepted verification debt, and continue with the remaining audit-floor reruns and closure gates without reopening implementation solely for this issue.

findings.title: Historical fail-first provenance remains unresolved verification debt
findings.rationale: Both the bounded package and the corrected-baseline rerun ledger explicitly state that preserved fail-first or red-run artifacts do not exist for RR-AUTH-04 because slice normalization happened after code and test hardening had already begun. The current packet substitutes named regression inventory plus focused, impacted-auth, architecture-guardrail, and full-suite rerun evidence, which is strong enough to reduce closure risk materially, but it still cannot prove these assertions would have failed on the pre-fix behavior.
findings.suggested_action: Carry this as explicit accepted verification debt in the acceptance ledger unless preserved red-run evidence can be reconstructed later; do not treat it as an implementation blocker because the corrected-baseline packet now provides broad, real-backend substitute coverage and a green full Laravel CI-equivalent suite.
